zh.Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, Engl: 英國巴金哈姆爾格倫登 安德伍德
zh.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Hadlow, Ken: 英國肯特哈德洛福聖 瑪麗 馮 維吉恩斯 邱奇
zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.Haudlo, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特哈德洛福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Haudlo於大約1334年出生在英國肯特哈德洛福時，她的父親Sir John Hadlow66歲，母親Maud Burnell45歲。她於大約1355年與Almaric de St. Amand在英國巴金哈姆爾格倫登 安德伍德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1383年1月27日在英國巴金哈姆爾格倫登 安德伍德享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Haudlo于大约1334年出生在英国肯特哈德洛福时，她的父亲Sir John Hadlow66岁，母亲Maud Burnell45岁。她于大约1355年与Almaric de St. Amand在英国巴金哈姆爾格倫登 安德伍德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1383年1月27日在英国巴金哈姆爾格倫登 安德伍德享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Haudlo was born about 1334, in Hadlow, Kent, England, her father, Sir John Hadlow, was 66 and her mother, Maud Burnell, was 45. She married Almaric de St. Amand about 1355, in Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 27 January 1383, in Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Haudlo est née environ 1334, à Hadlow, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Hadlow, avait 66 ans et sa mère, Maud Burnell, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Almaric de St. Amand environ 1355, à Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 27 janvier 1383, à Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Haudloが約1334年にイギリスケントハドロウで生まれた時，父Sir John Hadlowは66歳，母Maud Burnellは45歳であった。約1355年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアグレンドン ウンデルウッドでAlmaric de St. Amandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1383年1月27日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアグレンドン ウンデルウッドで50歳で死去した。

ko.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Hadlow, Ken: 영국 켄트 하들로 스트 메리 테 버긴'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Haudlo(이)가 대략 1334년 영국 켄트 하들로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Hadlow(은)는 66세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Burnell(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 대략 1355년 영국 벅잉함실르 그렌던 운더우드에서 Almaric de St. Amand(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1383년 1월 27일 영국 벅잉함실르 그렌던 운더우드에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Haudlo nasceu aproximadamente 1334, em Hadlow, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Hadlow, tinha 66 anos e sua mãe, Maud Burnell, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Almaric de St. Amand aproximadamente 1355, em Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 27 de janeiro de 1383, em Grendon Underwood, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 50 anos.

ru.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Hadlow, Ken: ст мэри т виргин'с чурч, хадлоу, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Haudlo родилась около 1334 г. в местности хадлоу, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Hadlow было 66 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Burnell, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Almaric de St. Amand около 1355 г. в местности грендон ундирвуд, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 27 января 1383 г. в местности грендон ундирвуд, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.
